heading: Procedure for issuance of orders
label: 80a–39 Procedure for issuance of orders
notes: Amendments1960—Subsec. (a). inserted “or certified mail” after “registered mail”.Pub. L. 86–507

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of Securities and Exchange Commission, with certain exceptions, to Chairman of such Commission, see Reorg. Plan No. 10 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff. , 15 F.R. 3175, , set out under .May 24, 195064 Stat. 1265section 78d of this title
amendments: Amendments1960—Subsec. (a). inserted “or certified mail” after “registered mail”.Pub. L. 86–507

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of Securities and Exchange Commission, with certain exceptions, to Chairman of such Commission, see Reorg. Plan No. 10 of 1950, §§ 1, 2, eff. , 15 F.R. 3175, , set out under .May 24, 195064 Stat. 1265section 78d of this title

children.heading: Notice and hearing
children.label: a Notice and hearing
children.text: Orders of the Commission under this subchapter shall be issued only after appropriate notice and opportunity for hearing. Notice to the parties to a proceeding before the Commission shall be given by personal service upon each party or by registered mail or certified mail or confirmed telegraphic notice to the party’s last known business address. Notice to interested persons, if any, other than parties may be given in the same manner or by publication in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Application verified under oath admissible as evidence
children.label: b Application verified under oath admissible as evidence
children.text: The Commission may provide, by appropriate rules or regulations, that an application verified under oath may be admissible in evidence in a proceeding before the Commission and that the record in such a proceeding may consist, in whole or in part, of such application.

children.heading: Parties
children.label: c Parties
children.text: In any proceeding before the Commission, the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as it may prescribe, shall admit as a party any interested State or State agency, and may admit as a party any representative of interested security holders, or any other person whose participation in the proceeding may be in the public interest or for the protection of investors.
